mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines > 0.5pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and declining inflation signals strong supply-side dynamics with stable pricing, creating a favorable environment for equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to explain 30%+ of SPX return variation in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with declining unemployment signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate profit growth and equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate elevated demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through its impact on related sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 40%+ of periods when HOUST exceeds 1500k units.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in consumer price inflation combined with rising federal funds rates signals an impending monetary tightening cycle that pressures equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong household demand that supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 40%+ of periods when RSAFS exceeds its 3-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening supply-side activity that reduces corporate earnings potential and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.0% in 1 month.
